duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Public _addCredIdPerAddress/_removeCredIdPerAddress allow any external caller to corrupt per-address cred tracking arrays, permanently DoS-ing share sells
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same access control defect on _addCredIdPerAddress/_removeCredIdPerAddress in Cred.sol leading to permanent share lock via index corruption

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Mint quantity (mintArgs_.quantity) is not included in the signed encodeData_ or Merkle leaf, allowing any valid proof holder to mint up to maxSupply
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same cryptographic binding defect where quantity is excluded from signature/proof verification in signatureClaim and merkleClaim. TECH2-H003 (second instance) and TECH2-H004 (second instance) also cover this same issue.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: buyShareCredFor/batchBuyShareCred sets lastTradeTimestamp for the curator_ parameter (not msg.sender), enabling continuous sell-lock griefing
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same vulnerability: third-party lock period reset via buyShareCredFor/batchBuyShareCred. ECON-H016 and ECON2-H012 specifically mention the batch variant but share the same root cause. TECH2-H004 (first instance) and TECH2-H007 (second instance) also target this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CuratorRewardsDistributor.distribute() has unbounded loop over all curators causing gas limit DoS and permanent reward lock
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same unbounded loop in distribute() over getCuratorAddresses causing block gas limit exhaustion. TECH2-H007 (first instance) is also in this group.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: RewardControl.withdrawFor lacks access control, allowing anyone to force-withdraw any user's reward balance
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing access control on withdrawFor(address, uint256) in RewardControl.sol enabling forced withdrawals

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PhiFactory.claim() self-call pattern traps excess ETH with no refund to original caller
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same ETH trapping mechanism in claim()/batchClaim() where this.merkleClaim{value:mintFee} forwards only exact fee and _processClaim refund goes to address(this). TECH2-H003 (first instance) and TECH2-H008 (second instance) also cover this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: credMerkleRoot[credChainId][credId] shared across all arts for same credId is overwritten when new art is created, invalidating existing Merkle claims
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same state corruption where _initializePhiArt overwrites the shared credMerkleRoot mapping, invalidating pending proofs for earlier arts. TECH2-H006 (first instance) targets this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Strategic share accumulation before distribute() call allows disproportionate curator reward capture
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same just-in-time share manipulation attack on CuratorRewardsDistributor.distribute() due to lack of time-weighted averaging or snapshot mechanism

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BondingCurve._getCreatorFee missing return statement at supply==0 causes incorrect price quotes in view functions
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing return statement in _getCreatorFee when supply_==0, causing getBuyPriceAfterFee/getSellPriceAfterFee to return inflated prices. TECH2-H009 (first instance) also targets this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Cred.createCred signature does not include block.chainid, enabling cross-chain replay
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing chainId in createCred signedData_ allowing cross-chain signature replay

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BondingCurve.setCredContract not called in deployment script, causing all trades to revert on address(0) calls
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same deployment configuration issue where bondingCurve.setCredContract is commented out in Deploy.s.sol

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Unbounded protocolFeePercent can cause sell payout underflow, trapping curator funds
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing upper bound on setProtocolFeePercent causing arithmetic underflow on sells when protocolFee + creatorFee >= price

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Individual buy/sell share functions lack nonReentrant modifier while performing external ETH transfers, enabling cross-function reentrancy
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same missing reentrancy guard on _handleTrade called by buyShareCred/sellShareCred with external ETH transfers creating reentrancy windows. TECH2-H006 (second instance) also targets this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: CuratorRewardsDistributor.distribute() sends royaltyfee + rounding dust to msg.sender (arbitrary caller) instead of protocol treasury
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same permissionless royalty extraction where distribute() sends withdrawRoyalty percentage to msg.sender. Both instances of TECH2-H005 target this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: BondingCurve._curve division by (TOTAL_SUPPLY_FACTOR - targetAmount_) can revert near asymptote at high supply
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same mathematical singularity in the bonding curve formula where denominator approaches zero as supply approaches TOTAL_SUPPLY_FACTOR/1 ether

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Artist can retroactively toggle soulBounded flag via updateArtSettings, locking or unlocking previously minted NFTs
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same issue where soulBounded is dynamically checked during transfer and can be changed post-minting by the art creator. TECH2-H010 (first instance) also targets this.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PhiFactory.claim() can use factory's own trapped ETH balance for minting when msg.value < mintFee due to self-call pattern
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same exploit where this.merkleClaim{value: mintFee} draws from the factory's own ETH balance, allowing free minting when the factory has accumulated trapped ETH

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: PhiRewards.handleRewardsAndGetValueSent has no access control, enabling fake reward event pollution and curator reward manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same missing access control on handleRewardsAndGetValueSent allowing arbitrary callers to deposit rewards for any addresses/credIds

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: getPositionsForCurator uses absolute index i (starting at start_) to write into array sized (stopIndex-start_), causing out-of-bounds revert when start_>0
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same array indexing bug in getPositionsForCurator where credIds[i] uses the loop variable directly instead of a relative index. TECH2-H010 (second instance) covers this same bug.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Claimable helper functions blindly forward msg.value and decoded calldata to PhiFactory, enabling griefing via malformed inputs
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same issue in Claimable.signatureClaim/merkleClaim where raw msg.data decoding and blind msg.value forwarding creates integration fragility. TECH2-H009 (second instance) covers this.
